article_title: Meta's Pyrefly Reaches 1.0, Bringing a Rust-Built Python Type Checker to Stable With 90% Spec Conformance

content_preview.title: Meta's Pyrefly Reaches 1.0, Bringing a Rust-Built Python Type Checker to Stable With 90% Spec Conformance
content_preview.summary: Pyrefly 1.0.0 ships on May 12, 2026 as Meta's production-grade Python type checker, checking 1.8 million lines per second and powering Instagram's codebase.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Meta released [Pyrefly 1.0.0](https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/releases/tag/1.0.0) on May 12, 2026, marking the graduation of its Rust-built Python type checker from beta to a stable, production-ready release. The tool processes 1.8 million lines of code per second, according to [Engineering at Meta](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/05/15/developer-tools/introducing-pyrefly-a-new-type-checker-and-ide-experience-for-python/), and is already the default type checker for developers w...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 791-word News article covering Pyrefly 1.0.0. Good use of concrete performance figures with appropriate sourcing. Architecture section provides useful technical context. Ecosystem comparison at the end places the release in broader Python tooling context.
editor_notes.source_verification: All five sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (GitHub release notes): confirmed 90%/70% conformance improvement, Django/factory_boy/Pydantic/PyTorch/Jupyter framework support, Instagram/PyTorch/JAX adoption. source-1.html.gz (Engineering at Meta): confirmed 1.8 million lines/sec, Rust implementation, MIT license, incremental module-based model. source-2.html.gz (InfoQ): confirmed 1.8 million lines/sec, 2.4s vs 35.2s vs 48.1s benchmark figures for PyTorch codebase. source-3.html.gz (JetBrains PyCharm blog): confirmed PyCharm 2026.1.2 integration, 0.5-1 second file resolution, Type widget in status bar. source-4.html.gz (InfoWorld): confirmed verbatim quotes 'broader existing feature set, better documentation, and tooling to allow elegant migration' and 'contextually detailed error messages'. No discrepancies found.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific figures verified. The 70% to 90%+ conformance improvement is from GitHub release notes. The 1.8M lines/sec figure comes from the Engineering at Meta blog (alpha period). The benchmark figures (2.4s/35.2s/48.1s) are from InfoQ's alpha-period coverage and are correctly attributed. Article correctly notes these are historical benchmarks.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Claims are properly attributed without editorializing. The competitive landscape is handled fairly.
editor_notes.originality: A February 2026 article mentioned Pyrefly in passing within a broader Python ecosystem piece, but the 1.0.0 release is distinct, timely news not previously covered. No duplicate.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, well-sourced News article covering a concrete software release milestone. All claims verified against primary sources. Ready for publication.
